_sources.discrepancies: NCSL lists AR as 'photo ID requested' (non-strict); SoS site confirms voters without photo ID may sign an affidavit and cast a regular ballot. Consistent.

earlyVoting.notes: Early voting begins 15 days before the election and ends the Saturday before. Hours vary by county. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting for runoff. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting begins 15 days before the election and ends the Saturday before. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting runs for 15 days before the election and ends the Saturday before Election Day. Hours vary by county.

acceptedIds: Arkansas driver's license or state ID card
Student ID from an Arkansas public college or university (with photo and address)
Any document issued by the federal, state, or local government that shows name and photo
votingRules.expirationRule: Photo ID must not be expired at time of voting. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: If a voter does not have an acceptable photo ID, they may sign an affidavit and cast a regular (non-provisional) ballot.
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: Arkansas prohibits use of wireless communication devices in the polling area. Voters may not photograph their ballot.
additionalRules: Open primary: any registered voter may choose which party's primary to participate in; party registration is not required.
Runoff rule: a voter who participated in one party's primary may only vote in that party's runoff.

eligibility: Will be unavoidably absent from the county on Election Day
Caregiving responsibilities for an ill or disabled person
Scheduled to work all poll hours on Election Day
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: Must be received by the county clerk 7 days before the election
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county clerk by 7:30 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Absentee ballot must arrive at the county clerk by poll closing on Election Day. Postmarks do not count.

runoffRules.ruleExplanation: Arkansas rule: if you voted in one party's primary, you can only vote in that same party's runoff. If you did not vote in the primary, you may choose either party's runoff.
